input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-3e17f116b8a94f498ad6378c76eb9e1a/Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net10.0 --nologo -- --runtimes net9.0 net10.0 --filter '*' 2>&1

data.reasoningText: BenchmarkDotNet needs to be run from one framework but can target multiple. I need to pick one framework to run from and use the `--runtimes` flag to target both. Let me use `--framework net10.0` and use `--runtimes net9.0 net10.0` to benchmark both.

data.content: The benchmark only ran on .NET 10. BenchmarkDotNet uses different moniker format. Let me fix this.
data.reasoningText:
It seems like BenchmarkDotNet only ran on .NET 10, not on both runtimes. Let me check - the `--runtimes` flag might not have worked as expected. Let me look at the full output more carefully. Actually, looking at the report header, it says `DefaultJob` only shows .NET 10.0.7. It didn't use `net9.0` runtime.

The issue is that `--runtimes net9.0 net10.0` with BenchmarkDotNet might need to have the correct moniker format. Let me try a different approach - re-run with explicit `Job` attributes or check the correct CLI syntax.


I think the runtime monikers need to be formatted differently for BenchmarkDotNet's CLI - trying `net90 net100` instead of the dotted versions might be the right syntax.

data.content: BDN 0.14 doesn't support .NET 10 multi-targeting. I'll simplify and run each TFM separately.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10 yet. Let me take a different approach - I'll run the benchmarks separately on each TFM rather than using BDN's multi-runtime support. I can either:

1. Use a newer BenchmarkDotNet version (0.14.x might have a newer patch)
2. Run benchmarks separately with `--framework net9.0` and `--framework net10.0`

Option 2 is simpler and will work. Let me simplify the config to just use the default job, and run separately for each framework.
